description:
You are a deployment engineer rolling out a dated snapshot of the application
  code that currently lives in

      /home/user/app/current

  Your job is to:

  1. Create a new release directory called
         /home/user/releases/2023-09-18
     (all intermediate directories must exist at the end of the task).

  2. Populate that directory with the *entire* contents of
         /home/user/app/current
     preserving filenames and file metadata (timestamps & permissions).

  3. Produce a compressed archive of the new release directory named
         /home/user/releases/2023-09-18.tgz
     The archive must sit directly in /home/user/releases and must contain
     *only* the single top-level directory 2023-09-18/ with its children.

  4. Append an entry to a plain-text file
         /home/user/deployment.log
     using the following exact, single-line format (no extra spaces):

         YYYY-MM-DD|SOURCE_DIR|TARGET_DIR|ARCHIVE_PATH

     For this task the line that must appear in the file is:

         2023-09-18|/home/user/app/current|/home/user/releases/2023-09-18|/home/user/releases/2023-09-18.tgz

     • If deployment.log already exists, add this entry on a **new line**
       at the end without changing previous lines.
     • If the file does not exist, create it containing only this one line
       (with a trailing newline).

  A quick self-check before finishing:
    • /home/user/releases/2023-09-18  exists and contains the same set of files
      that are present in /home/user/app/current.
    • /home/user/releases/2023-09-18.tgz  exists and, when listed with
      “tar -tzf”, shows paths beginning with  “2023-09-18/”.
    • /home/user/deployment.log  ends with the exact line specified above.

  Accomplish all of the above using normal user privileges.  You should only
  need a small handful of shell commands (< 5).  Do not hard-code paths other
  than those explicitly provided here.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing state supplied by the test harness -->
  1. Directory /home/user/app/current already exists.
  2. Inside that directory are two files:
       /home/user/app/current/app.py      (content: the single line "# placeholder\n")
       /home/user/app/current/README.md   (content: the single line "Demo app\n")
     Both files have permissions 0644.

  <!-- Expected final state after the agent completes the task -->
  • Directory /home/user/releases/2023-09-18 must exist and must contain
    app.py and README.md whose contents match byte-for-byte those in
    /home/user/app/current.

  • File /home/user/releases/2023-09-18.tgz must be a valid gzip-compressed
    tar archive.  When extracted, it yields one top-level directory
    “2023-09-18” that in turn contains app.py and README.md identical to their
    counterparts in /home/user/app/current.

  • File /home/user/deployment.log must exist.  Its final line
    (trimmed of trailing newline for comparison) must be exactly:
      2023-09-18|/home/user/app/current|/home/user/releases/2023-09-18|/home/user/releases/2023-09-18.tgz
    Previous lines (if any) must remain untouched.
